commit.message:
fix(player): block play_announcement until playback finishes

MA core keeps ANNOUNCEMENT_IN_PROGRESS set until play_announcement()
returns. Previously the method returned immediately, causing premature
volume restoration and state cleanup.

Now uses an asyncio.Event signaled by Glagol state updates:
- TTS: waits for aliceState SPEAKING → IDLE transition
- Audio: waits for playing to become False
- 30s timeout as safety fallback

Fix notes text not wrapping before button area stretches (#30)

Long notes text was pushing the right-side container wider than the
rating buttons row, instead of word-wrapping within the buttons' width.

The right-side container was a plain `<div>` whose width was determined
by its widest child. When notes exceeded the buttons row width, the
entire container grew.

- **`vote-song-list.tsx`**: Make the right-side container `flex w-min
flex-col items-end` — `w-min` (`width: min-content`) constrains the
column width to the `shrink-0` rating buttons' intrinsic width, forcing
note text to word-wrap within that width
- **`note-editor.tsx`**: Add `self-stretch` to both display and editing
containers so they fill the column width, and add `break-words` to the
note paragraph for safety with very long words

commit.message:
refactor(jetson): migrate from Docker to host-native deployment

NVIDIA does not provide an official JetPack 6 Docker base image with
TRT 10.x + Python 3.10. The existing Dockerfile targets JetPack 5
(TRT 8.5, Python 3.8, CUDA 11.4) which is incompatible with JetPack 6
hardware. TensorRT engines are not portable across TRT versions.

Changes:
- setup_jetson.sh: Remove Docker build/run, add Python/TRT/PyCUDA
  verification, generate host-native systemd service with sandbox
  hardening (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=strict, PrivateTmp)
- setup_jetson.sh: Replace world-writable Docker perms (777/666)
  with standard user perms (755/644), add final chown after model
  conversion to fix root-owned files
- Dockerfile: Mark as DEPRECATED with explanation
- requirements.txt: Bump flask 3.1.3, waitress 3.0.2 (host Python 3.10)
- agrikd.service: Update template comments
- app/main.py, app/gui_app.py: Update path resolution comments
- All docs updated: jetson_deployment_guide.md, project_instruction.md,
  build_plan.md, project_context.md, docs/README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add npm install step before npm publish in release workflow (#7473)

The prepare script runs husky, which is a devDependency. Without running
npm install first, husky won't be available and npm publish will fail.

commit.message:
fix: use Modifier::BOLD for command names in overlays to force brightness

Style::default() renders as dim on Catppuccin and similar dark color
schemes where the terminal's base foreground color is desaturated.
BOLD reliably renders bright on all terminals regardless of palette.

- SelectionList non-selected label: default() → BOLD
- SelectionList non-selected desc: DIM → default() (visually secondary to bold label)
- InfoPanel /help command names: BOLD (unchanged) + desc: DIM → default()
- Tip lines in InfoPanel: keep DIM (truly secondary info)

commit.message:
fix(ci): stabilize main CI — signing verification, deploy gating, audit decoupling (#310)

Persistent CI failures on `main` (#238, #278, #302) traced to the
`verify-commit-signatures` job rejecting legitimate bot commits,
silently swallowing transient API failures, and cascading into a blocked
`security-audit` job. Additionally, `railway-deploy.yml` runs in
parallel with CI, allowing deployment before CI passes.

### Signing verification fixes

- **Add `dependabot[bot]` and `github-actions[bot]` to
`.github/trusted_bot_logins`** — these were simply missing, causing
every Dependabot PR and workflow auto-commit to fail signature
verification
- **Retry `is_github_verified_commit` up to 3 times** — the previous
implementation silently treated transient API failures (empty response)
as `verified=false`, failing the entire CI run on a network blip
- **Add Pattern 2 to `is_trusted_bot_commit`** — the existing check
required `committer_login == "web-flow"`, which doesn't hold for commits
pushed directly by workflow automation (e.g. supabase-typegen). New
pattern accepts commits where both author and committer are trusted bot
logins with noreply emails

```bash
# Pattern 1 (existing): Bot → web-flow committer → unsigned
# Pattern 2 (new):      Bot → trusted-bot committer → noreply email
if [[ "$committer_email" =~ ^[0-9]+\+[^@]+@users\.noreply\.github\.com$ ||
      "$committer_email" == "noreply@github.com" ]]; then
  is_trusted_login "$committer_login" "$trusted_logins_file" && return 0
fi
```

### CI workflow fixes

- **Decouple `security-audit` from `verify-commit-signatures`** in
`ci.yml` — dependency scanning and commit provenance are independent
concerns; a signing hiccup should not block vulnerability scanning
- **Add `ci-gate` job to `railway-deploy.yml`** — polls the CI workflow
status for up to 20 minutes before allowing any deploy job to start,
preventing deployment of code that hasn't passed CI

### Diagnostic improvements

- `check-commit-signatures.sh` now logs: API availability at startup,
per-commit trust classification (TRUSTED-SIGNED / LEGACY-EXCEPTION /
GITHUB-VERIFIED / TRUSTED-BOT / UNTRUSTED), and total commit count —
makes future failures immediately diagnosable from CI logs

### Post-merge triage

| Failure class | Action |
|---|---|
| Dependabot PRs failing only on signing | Rebase and retest |
| Dependabot PRs failing on tests | Branch-specific dep incompatibility
— review individually |
| Open signing-policy fix branches | Superseded by this PR — close |

---------

commit.message:
Fix OpenAPI publish job being skipped on manual workflow dispatch (#45)

https://github.com/SubDynamics/event-hub-api/pull/45

The OpenAPI workflow’s `publish-types-package` job could be skipped on
manual runs due to an overly strict branch gate and string-based input
check. This updates job gating so manual dispatch behaves independently
from push-to-main change detection.

- **Condition split by trigger type**
- Kept push behavior scoped to `main` + detected `packages/types/**`
changes.
- Separated manual dispatch logic so it no longer depends on `github.ref
== refs/heads/main`.

- **Typed manual input evaluation**
- Switched from `github.event.inputs.publish == 'true'` to
`inputs.publish` to align with boolean `workflow_dispatch` inputs.

- **Resulting job predicate**
  ```yaml
  if: >
    (github.event_name == 'push' &&
    github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
    needs.generate-and-validate.outputs.types_changed == 'true') ||
    (github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && inputs.publish)
  ```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SubDynamics <23660334+SubDynamics@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use Style::default()+DIM for overlay text to avoid ANSI color remapping

Replace Color::White and MUTED in overlays with terminal-adaptive styles:
- Primary text: Style::default() (inherits terminal's configured fg color)
- Secondary text: Modifier::DIM (dims relative to terminal's native color)

Affected: SelectionList labels/descs/markers, InfoPanel help lines (commands,
tips), status overlay values, and all overlay border hint text.

than true-color Rgb(140,140,140), producing inverted visual hierarchy.

Add step segmentation protocol

Implement hierarchical step segmentation as described in Appendix:
- Priority 1: Structural delimiters (\n\n, \n, \\)
- Priority 2: Logic connectors (Therefore, Thus, Hence, So, Consequently)

- LaTeX math protection (never split inside $...$)
- Max 256 tokens per step to prevent run-on steps

commit.message:
fix: use npm install instead of npm ci for frontend in CI

The lockfile was generated with npm 11.x but CI runs npm 10.x
(Node 22 default). npm ci strictly validates the lockfile and
fails on optional peer dependency resolution differences between
npm versions (esbuild@0.28.0 via vitest/vite 8).

Using npm install is more lenient and handles cross-version
lockfile compatibility.

commit.message:
fix(jetson): bind-mount host TensorRT/PyCUDA into Docker container

L4T runtime image does not include TensorRT Python bindings or PyCUDA.
Instead of trying to install them inside the container (which needs
nvcc/CUDA dev headers not in runtime image), bind-mount the host's
pre-installed packages into the container at runtime.

setup_jetson.sh now:
- Auto-detects host site-packages location for tensorrt/pycuda
- Adds -v bind-mount flags to the Docker run command in systemd service
- Falls back to mounting full dist-packages if specific dirs not found

Also:
- Add ENV PATH/LD_LIBRARY_PATH in Dockerfile for CUDA visibility
- Make check_ready() create DB dir if missing (os.makedirs)
- Revert pycuda from requirements.txt (host-provided, not pip)
